title: Neocodon altaicus

The health benefits of Neocodon altaicus include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wound healing, fever reduction, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neocodon altaicus include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Neocodon altaicus is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Neocodon altaicus is classified as a species within the genus Neocodon, which is a part of the family Boraginaceae. This family is commonly referred to as the borage or forget-me-not family, a group of flowering plants native to various parts of the world. Within the Boraginaceae family, Neocodon altaicus can be further grouped into the order Boraginales, along with other species of borage and forget-me-not plants. Neocodon altaicus is also classified as a flowering plant, belonging to the kingdom Plantae.
